title: QUOTA-PAUSE wake-at is always empty: QuotaBoundaryCheckpointer writes QuotaResetAt="" so auto-resume scheduling math has no timestamp
summary: Verified 2026-07-13: go/internal/checkpoint/checkpoint.go:192 — the all-families-exhausted checkpointer (cycle-656 seam) writes ReasonQuotaLikely with QuotaResetAt left empty; cmd_loop_control.go:309-316 then emits 'QUOTA-PAUSE: ... wake-at= source=unknown'. skills/loop/SKILL.md:203-204 instructs the operator model to parse wake-at=ISO8601 and compute the ScheduleWakeup delay from it — math that can never run on the current Go path. Fix: wire the existing reset estimation into the checkpointer — usageprobe.ProbeQuota buckets carry ResetAt (quotastate.TightestRemaining) and quotareset.Compute provides the fallback estimate (operator override > hint file > now+DefaultHours); populate QuotaResetAt + QuotaResetSource at checkpoint-write time. Keep fail-open: no probe data => explicit source=estimate with the default-hours fallback rather than empty.
